"Hal ta'lamu lahu samiyya" — when God has no peer, parallel, or equal, who is there to obstruct His plans or stay His hand? Continue your task; He will make every difficulty easy and bring all His plans to fruition.

وَيَقُولُ الإنْسَانُ أَئِذَا مَا مِتُّ لَسَوْفَ أُخْرَجُ حَيًّا۔ 

Gabriel's speech is completed. Now, this is directly from Allah Almighty. After exhorting the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) to patience and steadfastness, the primary cause of the opponents' denial is referenced in their own words, followed by its refutation. Although the word "insan" (human) here is general, contextual clues show that it refers to the Arab mushrikeen, who considered the Resurrection to be far-fetched and denied it. Speaking of them with a general word carries the eloquence of showing that they are not worthy of specific attention, which is why a general form was used.

The stance of the Arab mushrikeen regarding the Resurrection, as we have explained elsewhere, was not one of open denial; rather, they were caught in a contradiction. They accepted Allah as the One Who gives life and causes death, but they considered it highly far-fetched that after dying and turning to dust, all people would be raised alive again and face accountability. Their leaders and wealthy class were also in the misunderstanding that if the Resurrection occurred, they would receive there the same things they possessed here. Above all, their greatest trust was in their self-made deities; they worshipped the angels, considering them God's daughters, and their presumption was that if the Resurrection occurred and they faced accountability, these deities would rescue them from God. Keep these contradictory views of the mushrikeen in mind to understand the subsequent discussions in the Qur'an.

أَوَلا يَذْكُرُ الإنْسَانُ أَنَّا خَلَقْنَاهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَلَمْ تَكُ شَيْئًا 

**A Self-Evident Mental Contradiction:** Attention is drawn to this mental contradiction: when this human cannot deny the reality that God created him from absolute non-existence, why does he find it far-fetched that He will raise him up again after death? When He can create from "nothing", why should it be difficult for Him to recreate from "something"?

فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَحْشُرَنَّهُمْ وَالشَّيَاطِينَ ثُمَّ لَنُحْضِرَنَّهُمْ حَوْلَ جَهَنَّمَ جِثِيًّا 

By "Shayateen" (devils) here, contextual clues show that both the devils among the jinn and the devils among humans are meant. We have mentioned elsewhere regarding the Arabs that they worshipped many jinns. Similarly, their misleading leaders misled them, and they followed them blindly, and when Allah's Messenger invited them to open their eyes, they sought to harm him.

"Jithiyya" is the plural of "jathin". "Jatha yajthoo" means to sit on one's knees or hams. This is the posture of criminals. Just as criminals sit before a ruler to hear their judgment, this word describes such a submissive and defeated posture.

Now, swearing with full emphasis, it is stated: "By your Lord, We will gather them and all the devils they worshipped and obeyed, and all their misleading leaders they followed, around Hell in such a manner that they will be sitting on their knees like criminals, waiting for the judgment of which ward of Hell they are ordered to enter."

ثُمَّ لَنَنْزِعَنَّ مِنْ كُلِّ شِيعَةٍ أَيُّهُمْ أَشَدُّ عَلَى الرَّحْمَنِ عِتِيًّا۔ It is stated: After this, We will separate and extract from every group and party those of their leaders who were most vehement in opposing God and His Messenger and running campaigns against Him. They will be separated because, just as they led the campaigns to mislead people in the world, they will now lead their followers into Hell; they will be in front, and their followers behind, and whoever is worthy of whichever level of Hell will enter it.

ثُمَّ لَنَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِالَّذِينَ هُمْ أَوْلَى بِهَا صِلِيًّا۔ It is stated: At that time, we alone will know who is most worthy of entering this Hell, who should enter first, and into which level. No one else will know these things better than Us, such that We would need to consult anyone regarding anyone, or that anyone would be in a position to claim they know more than Us and thus their recommendation on behalf of someone is worthy of attention. This is a subtle reproach to the mushrikeen's false belief in intercession: that intercession before God can only be made by one who claims to know more about someone than God. Who could be so self-deluded as to tell Allah Almighty: "You do not know so-and-so, but I do; he is a very righteous man, so do not say anything to him, but send him straight to Paradise"!

وَإِنْ مِنْكُمْ إِلا وَارِدُهَا كَانَ عَلَى رَبِّكَ حَتْمًا مَقْضِيًّا۔ "Hatm" means obligatory and binding. The pronoun of address refers to the very criminals who have been mentioned above. The previous discourse was spoken in the third person (absent), and this is addressed directly to them. Both styles carry distinct benefits. Just as the third-person style is evidence of indifference, the second-person style (address) is evidence of severe anger. Examples of this in the Qur'an and Arabic speech are many, where the style suddenly shifts from third-person to direct address. Here too, such a shift has occurred. Since the purpose is to express severe anger, Allah will address the criminals, saying: "Now the time for pleading, lamenting, and making excuses has passed; now, without exception, every one of you must enter this Hell." At the same time, the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) is reassured that this matter is completely absolute and decided; your Lord has made it binding upon Himself. One day you will see this end of your enemies with your own eyes.

This interpretation of the verses is completely clear, but our commentators have taken the addressees to mean all of humanity, thereby making it necessary for every person, whether righteous or wicked, to pass through Hell. The only saving grace they offer is that they claim there will be a bridge over Hell named "Sirat", over which the righteous will pass, while the wicked will be left to fall into Hell. This misunderstanding occurred to the commentators solely due to a failure to understand the style of the text, and we have seen that most of their slips are the result of this very thing. It is surprising that while making such a harsh claim, these gentlemen did not recall the verse of Surah Al-Anbiya regarding the righteous: "Indeed, those for whom the best [reward] has preceded from Us—they are from it far removed. They will not hear its hissing, while they are in that which their souls desire abiding eternally. They will not be grieved by the greatest terror, and the angels will meet them, [saying], 'This is your Day which you were promised.'" (Al-Anbiya: 101-103)

To remain safe from such errors of interpretation, our advice to those who ponder the Qur'an is to study Maulana Hamiduddin Farahi's book *Asaleeb al-Qur'an* with a deep eye.

ثُمَّ نُنَجِّي الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا وَنَذُرُ الظَّالِمِينَ فِيهَا جِثِيًّا۔ "Thumma" denotes order. That is, Allah will first deal with the wrongdoers, and after sending them into Hell, He will turn to those who feared Him. It is stated: "Then We will save those who feared Us, and leave the wrongdoers in it, sitting on their knees." "We will save" does not mean We will save them from Hell itself; the heat of Hell will not even touch the godfearing, as passed above. Here, "saving" means saving them from all the anxieties, worries, and waiting which, in any case, confront the people of truth before reaching their final destination.

**Dispelling a Doubt:** In "wa nadharu-dhalimeena feeha jithiyya", the research on the word "jithiyy" passed above. "Jithiyy" refers to those who sit on their knees like criminals, waiting for the decision of their fate. In the previous verse, its use was completely clear, but here its use strikes us because "feeha" (in it) indicates that this is their state *inside* Hell; but inside it, their weeping and wailing should be mentioned, as in other places, rather than sitting on their knees. To escape this difficulty, our commentators and translators have altered its meaning, but this alteration is of the nature of exceeding the language, which is why we do not agree with it. In our view, this state is indeed described inside Hell, but this relates to the very initial stage when, after the divine judgment, they are handed over to the keepers of Hell and sit on their knees waiting for the gates of punishment to open. Allah will hand them over to the keepers of Hell and leave them in this state, turning away from them, after which the gates of punishment will be opened upon them.

وَإِذَا تُتْلَى عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُنَا بَيِّنَاتٍ قَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَيُّ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ خَيْرٌ مَقَامًا وَأَحْسَنُ نَدِيًّا۔ This clarifies the mindset of those arrogant people: when the clear, evidence-based verses warning them of reward, punishment, and the torment of this world and the Hereafter are recited to them, they make this boast to the believers with great boldness: "Tell us, in terms of status, position, power, wealth, assembly, and society, whose rank is higher—yours or ours? If our rank is higher—and it is self-evidently higher—then how can we believe that we are worthy of God's anger and that any punishment is going to come upon us? We see that in the eyes of God, we possess more honor than you, as He has granted us all this, while you are deprived of all these things."

وَكَم| أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُمْ مِنْ قَرْنٍ هُمْ أَحْسَنُ أَثَاثًا وَرِئْيًا۔ "Qarn" means the people of an era, a nation, or a generation. "R'y" means appearance, view, splendor, and grandeur.

**Response to the Arrogant Boast:** In response to those making this boast, it is stated: An abundance of wealth and splendor is neither proof of a nation being beloved to God, nor do these things save one from God's grasp. History contains many examples of nations that were far superior to them in terms of wealth and outward splendor, yet when they rebelled against God, they were destroyed. Only a reference to those nations is made here; their details are present in other Surahs.

قُلْ مَنْ كَانَ فِي الضَّلالَةِ فَلْيَمْدُدْ لَهُ الرَّحْمَنُ مَدًّا حَتَّى إِذَا رَأَوْا مَا يُوعَدُونَ إِمَّا الْعَذَابَ وَإِمَّا السَّاعَةَ فَسَيَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ هُوَ شَرٌّ مَكَانًا وَأَضْعَفُ جُنْدًا 

**The Divine Way Regarding Rebels:** This describes the divine way (Sunnah) that Allah adopts regarding His disobedient and rebellious servants. It is stated: Tell those who are mocking your warning in the vanity of their worldly superiority: "For those who choose to remain in misguidance rather than choosing the path of guidance, Allah's way is not to snatch away their luxury or seize them immediately; rather, the demand of His mercy is to give them maximum respite so they may fill their cup of sin, and His proof may be established against them. God is not a weak entity who fears that if He does not seize them immediately, His prey will escape. His plan is exceptionally firm, which is why He continues to lengthen the rope, until either the punishment descends upon them—which is the inevitable result of denying the messenger—or the Resurrection itself appears, of which they are being warned. At that time, they will realize who is worse in position and weaker in forces."

**Points Worthy of Attention:** Two or three points should be noted in this verse. First, the style of "fal-yamdud". It is not said: "God lengthens their rope," but: "it is fitting for the Lord of Mercy to lengthen their rope"—meaning that granting respite to the wrongdoers is a demand of God's power, wisdom, and plan. Unfortunate are those who take this respite as their success and become more daring in rebellion. Second, "punishment" and "the Hour" (Resurrection) are mentioned together. When these two words are used together, one refers to the punishment of this world, and the other to the punishment of the Hereafter. Allah's messengers warned their respective nations of both these punishments. We have mentioned elsewhere that if a nation denies its messenger, it is inevitably destroyed after proof is established. Third, comparative forms (tafdheel) are sometimes used stripped of the meaning of comparison. We have explained this elsewhere.

It should also be remembered here that just as the fools of Arab jahiliyyah, in the intoxication of their worldly success, did not consider the matters of God and the Hereafter worthy of attention, the intellectuals of modern science, in the same pride, declare God and the Hereafter to be an illusion. Recently, a friend mentioned that if you mention God and the Hereafter to people in America, they reply: "Our average life expectancy is higher than yours. If what you say were true, your life expectancy should have been higher than ours." I said: "Their average life expectancy is indeed high, and their suicide rate is also exceptionally high. They still have not been able to banish the Angel of Death from their land; the only difference is that they have taken his job into their own hands!"

وَيَزِيدُ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ اهْتَدَوْا هُدًى وَالْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ خَيْرٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ مَرَدًّا 

**Glad Tidings to the Believers:** This verse stands in contrast to verse 75 above. That is, just as Allah lengthens the rope of those who prefer misguidance over guidance, He continuously increases the guidance of those who choose guidance; and in the eyes of a wise person, the thing to observe is not what the immediate benefit of an action is, but which action is better in terms of its reward and final end. The worshippers of the world observe only the immediate benefit, without concerning themselves with what will happen after death. In contrast, the focus of a believer is on the future—that is, the Hereafter—and he evaluates his every action from the perspective of its final end. In this verse, the believers are consoled: "Do not care about the taunts of those who boast of their worldly means; though you are empty-handed, your guidance is increasing moment by moment, and this treasure of yours is the guarantee of your eternal kingdom, while the means gathered by those who taunt you will serve as fuel to burn them." I have translated "marad" as "final end", and this translation is closest to the true spirit of the word.

**The Expression "Baqiyat-us-Salihat" (The Enduring Righteous Deeds):** In the Qur'an, righteous deeds are referred to in various places as "Baqiyat-us-Salihat". This points to the truth that only righteous deeds are durable and immortal, while deeds that are temporary and mortal are unrighteous. As for the question of which deeds are enduring and which are mortal, the answer is: deeds performed with only this world as the objective are mortal, because this world itself is mortal. Enduring deeds are only those performed with God and the Hereafter as the objective, because God is immortal and the Hereafter is also immortal.

Tafseer of Verses 77 to 78: أَفَرَأَيْتَ الَّذِي كَفَرَ بِآيَاتِنَا وَقَالَ لأوتَيَنَّ مَالا وَوَلَدًا۔ اَطَّلَعَ الْغَيْبَ اَمِ اتَّخَذَ عِنْدَ الرَّحْمٰنِ عَهْدًا۔ This refers to another misconception of those who deny, and refutes it. This misconception is referenced with the address "afara'ayta" (have you seen). Those with a taste for the Arabic language know that when a discourse begins with this address, it means that a reference to some highly self-deluded person or some highly absurd statement is to follow.

Here, keep in mind also the style of Arabic where "alladhi" (the one who) is not necessarily used for a specific, defined person, but sometimes for characterization, of which highly eloquent examples exist in the Qur'an and Arabic speech. In this case, no specific individual is meant; rather, the purpose is to depict a specific character or mindset. Here too, it is not used for a specific person, but represents the mindset of a specific group. It is stated: "Observe this self-deluded person who is caught in the illusion that if the Resurrection occurs, he will be granted wealth and children there just as he has been here." People of this mindset do not consider blessings to be a gift of God, but a result of their own personal right or capability, which is why they remain in the vanity that they are the natural owners of leadership and wealth; who can deprive them of this? If there is such a thing as the Hereafter, they will live in palaces and drive cars there too.

**Refutation through Sarcasm:** "Attala'al-ghayba amittakhadha 'indar-Rahmani 'ahda." Since this presumption is completely foolish and childish, the response is delivered in a tone of sarcasm and contempt. It is stated: "Has he peeked into the veils of the unseen to see all the things he is to receive in the Hereafter, or has he obtained a written guarantee from God? On what basis is this vanity!"

Tafseer of Verses 79 to 80: كَلا سَنَكْتُبُ مَا يَقُولُ وَنَمُدُّ لَهُ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ مَدًّا (٧٩) وَنَرِثُهُ مَا يَقُولُ وَيَأْتِينَا فَرْدًا (٨٠)

**Refutation in a Tone of Wrath:** This refutes this false presumption in a tone of wrath. It is stated: "We will record his nonsense, and increase his punishment in retribution for it." That is, the punishment to be given for disbelief and denial will be given anyway, but a further increase will be made due to this arrogant claim. "Wa narithuhu ma yaqoolu wa ya'teena fardan" — meaning: "We will be the inheritor of all those things he claims and boasts of, and he will present himself before Us on the Day of Resurrection entirely alone; neither his wealth, his forces, nor his presumed partners and intercessors will be with him. Whatever he was granted was granted by Us; We will take it all back, and just as he entered the world empty-handed, he will return to Us empty-handed."

Tafseer of Verses 81 to 82: وَاتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ آلِهَةً لِيَكُونُوا لَهُمْ عِزًّا (٨١) كَلا سَيَكْفُرُونَ بِعِبَادَتِهِم| وَيَكُونُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضِدًّا 

The greatest support of these mushrikeen was their false deities. Their presumption was that if they had to present themselves before God and face accountability, these deities would save them through their power and influence. Specifically, they had great trust in the intercession of the angels. They presumed them to be God's daughters and worshipped them with the idea that God would not reject their word. In this world, they would intercede with their father to grant them provision and children, and if any need arose after death, they would serve as a source of strength and support there too. This strength and support is referred to here as "'Izz". Its original meaning is strength. "Kalla sayakfuroona bi-ibadatihim wa yakoonoona 'alayhim dhidda" — this refutes their false presumption. It is stated: "This desire of these fools will never be fulfilled; rather, on the Day of Resurrection, these deities of theirs will openly deny their worship." The Qur'an specifies that when the mushrikeen call upon their deities on the Day of Resurrection, saying: "We worshipped you in the world, so help us here," they will immediately reply: "We had no idea that some fools were worshipping us. When did we tell you to worship us? If you committed this foolishness, face its consequences." "Wa yakoonoona 'alayhim dhidda" — meaning: they hold these desires that their deities will serve as strength and support, but the matter will be the exact opposite; they will instead curse them and become their enemies.

Tafseer of Verses 83 to 84: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّا أَرْسَلْنَا الشَّيَاطِينَ عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ تَؤُزُّهُمْ أَزًّا (٨٣) فَلا تَعْجَلْ عَلَيْهِمْ إِنَّمَا نَعُدُّ لَهُمْ عَدًّا 

In Arabic, when they say "arsala-kalba 'alas-sayd", it means he unleashed the dog upon the prey. In this style, it is stated here: "We have unleashed the devils upon these disbelievers, so they may incite them as much as they can against the call of truth." "Azza ya'uzzu" means to incite, inflame, and provoke. This treatment was meted out to these disbelievers in accordance with the divine way mentioned in Surah Az-Zukhruf: "And whoever is blinded from remembrance of the Most Merciful—We appoint for him a devil, and he is to him a companion." (Az-Zukhruf: 36)

**Solace and Reassurance to the Prophet:** This is solace and reassurance from Allah to the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him): "Do not be alarmed or distressed by the opposition of these opponents, and do not hasten for the manifestation of the divine judgment regarding them. The more intense their opposition becomes, the closer they are drawing to their destruction. Due to their enmity to the truth, We have now unleashed the devils upon them, so they may incite them as much as they can, so that Our proof may be established against them." "Innama na'uddu lahum 'adda" — meaning: "There is no delay now in the manifestation of their judgment. Their cup is about to overflow, and the hand of the clock is reaching its final point. We are counting every single minute with complete care, and once the time is complete, We will not grant them respite for a single moment."

Recall that this set of verses began with Gabriel's exhortation of patience and waiting to the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him). The same subject is expressed here in another manner directly from Allah, containing a final warning for the opponents and glad tidings of a swift resolution of the matter for the Prophet. The words "ta'uzzuhum azza" and "fala ta'jal 'alayhim" also make it clear that these verses were revealed at a time when the opposition of the opponents had reached its peak, to the extent that the Prophet too was waiting at all times for the resolution of this conflict.

Tafseer of Verses 85 to 87: يَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ الْمُتَّقِينَ إِلَى الرَّحْمَنِ وَفْدًا (٨٥) وَنَسُوقُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ إِلَى جَهَنَّمَ وِرْدًا (٨٦) لا يَمْلِكُونَ الشَّفَاعَةَ إِلا مَنِ اتَّخَذَ عِنْدَ الرَّحْمَنِ عَهْدًا (٨٧)

**The Meaning of "Wafd":** "Wafd" means to go somewhere with honor and respect, just as ambassadors and envoys go to a king or ruler. "Wird" is a noun from "warada yaridu", meaning to descend upon a watering place, just as thirsty camels go to a watering place. In "illa manittakhadha 'indar-Rahmani 'ahda", the exception in my view is disconnected (munqati'). This is a negation of the presumed intercession of these criminals. It is stated: "On the Day We gather the righteous with honor before the Lord of Mercy, and drive these criminals like thirsty camels to the watering place of Hell, their presumed intercessors will possess no power of intercession. This right will belong only to those who have secured it from Allah."

**Some Fundamental Points Regarding Intercession:** Who are the ones who will possess this right? We have discussed this question at appropriate places in this book. Let us remind ourselves of some fundamental points:

1. The status of intercession will belong only to the prophets and the martyrs of the Ummah; this is an office of honor and dignity with which Allah will bless only those who are worthy of this honor.

2. These people too will intercede only with God's permission, and only for those for whom Allah grants permission. They will not take the initiative to intercede of their own accord, nor will they intercede for those for whom they have not received permission.

3. In their intercession, they will say only what is entirely true. Turning falsehood into truth or vice into virtue is neither fitting for them, nor can anyone dare such a thing before the Knower of the Unseen.

4. For those who lived a life of shirk and atheism, or claimed faith but spent their entire lives in falsehood and disobedience to God and His Messenger, there is no intercession.

All these points are established from the texts of the Qur'an itself. We have discussed them in various places and will discuss them in detail further on, if Allah wills. Obviously, when all these conditions apply to intercession and cannot be denied, no room remains to use intercession as a license for committing sins. In that case, hope of intercession can be held only by those who spent their lives in faith, righteous deeds, repentance, and reform, but due to weakness, negligence, or being overcome by emotions, committed errors alongside their good deeds. Such people may hope to be worthy of Allah's mercy and be forgiven through the Prophet's intercession. As for those who, on the strength of intercession, have made sins their very lifestyle, and like the Jews hope that "we will be forgiven; we are the forgiven Ummah, all our sins will be pardoned," no room exists for this self-delusion in the light of the Qur'an.

وَقَالُوا اتَّخَذَ الرَّحْمَنُ وَلَدًا۔ 

"Walad" means offspring. It is used for singular, plural, masculine, and feminine. In the matter of attributing offspring to God, the Arab mushrikeen, the Jews, and the Christians are all alike. The Arab mushrikeen called the angels the daughters of God; the Jews believed Ezra to be the son of God; and what the Christians made of the Messiah has passed in detail earlier in this Surah. In the previous verse, false intercession was refuted. Now, in the subsequent verses, this hideous form of shirk is refuted, which in reality came into existence under the same concept of false intercession to which we referred. Obviously, if one wishes to obtain a free license for their crimes from God, this cannot be done without assuming some sons and daughters for God, worshipping them, and hoping that no matter what we do, these beloved ones of God will not let Him lay His hands on us. Consequently, the Arabs made the angels the daughters of God under this desire, and the Christians invented the doctrine that God sacrificed His beloved son for our sake, making him an atonement for all our sins.

لَقَدْ جِئْتُمْ شَيْئًا إِدًّا۔ "Idda" means a harsh, severe, and grave thing. Note here also the style where the discourse was in the third person, but when the moment of severe anger arrived, the second-person style of direct address appeared. This is the same style we mentioned under "wa in minkum illa wariduha".

It is stated: "You have committed an exceptionally grave thing." Grave because this is associating another as partner and equal in God's divinity and uniqueness; and God is self-respecting (Ghayoor), which is why He can never tolerate His uniqueness being tarnished by declaring anyone as His equal. When humans, being mere servants, do not tolerate anything that challenges their self-respect, how can the Self-Respecting God, Who is the sole Owner of the entire heavens and earth, tolerate His servants worshipping anyone other than Him? To express God's self-respect, ancient scriptures have used the parable: just as you do not tolerate your wife sleeping in the arms of another, how can the Self-Respecting God tolerate His servant prostrating to another?

تَكَادُ السَّمَاوَاتُ يَتَفَطَّرْنَ مِنْهُ وَتَنْشَقُّ الأرْضُ وَتَخِرُّ الْجِبَالُ هَدًّا۔ 

"Hadda" means the falling of a wall or similar structure with a loud crash.

**Expressing the Gravity of Shirk:** This clarifies the gravity of this matter: that the heavens are almost torn apart because of it, the earth split open, and the mountains fallen down with a crash. This shows that the heavens, earth, and mountains also possess such self-respect and zeal regarding this accusation that they do not wish to tolerate these wicked people who associate partners with God for a single minute, but they are subject to the command of the Lord of Mercy, which is why they suppress their anger as long as He grants respite to a group.

**A Statement of Reality:** It should be clear that this is not an exaggerated style of expression, but a statement of reality. When a self-respecting son cannot tolerate someone attributing him to a father other than his own, or associating others as his father, how can the heavens, earth, seas, and mountains tolerate this disgrace of attributing them to anyone other than the One God, or associating others as partners in their creation? We have explained elsewhere that the nature of the heavens, earth, sun, and moon is Abrahamic. They express this natural state through their manifestations, and this nature of theirs refuses to tolerate those who attribute offspring to God; but their reins are in the hand of God, which is why they cannot take any step of their own will.

أَنْ دَعَوْا لِلرَّحْمَنِ وَلَدًا۔ This describes the cause of the anger and wrath of the heavens, earth, and mountains: that their wrath is because people have attributed offspring to the Lord of Mercy.

وَمَا يَنْبَغِي لِلرَّحْمَنِ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ وَلَدًا۔ This refutes the misguidance of attributing offspring to God from the perspective of its being incompatible with His divine attributes: "What need does He have to take a son? He needs neither partner nor helper in His tasks, nor any heir for His properties, nor is He dependent on anyone to keep His name alive, nor does He require any support in old age. He is rich and independent in His essence; for what purpose then would He make offspring?"

إِنْ كُلُّ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأرْضِ إِلا آتِي الرَّحْمَنِ عَبْدًا۔ Now, this clarifies the true status of His entire creation: everyone in the heavens and the earth—whether angels, jinn, or humans, and whatever their rank or status, whether small or great—will without exception present themselves before the Lord of Mercy as a servant. In terms of being servants, all will be equal. There will be neither son nor daughter of God there.

Tafseer of Verses 94 to 95: لَقَدْ أَحْصَاهُمْ وَعَدَّهُمْ عَدًّا (٩٤) وَكُلُّهُمْ آتِيهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فَرْدًا (٩٥)

"Ihsa'" originally means to keep in check and control.

Now, this clarifies the nature of everyone's presence on the Day of Resurrection: everyone is under God's complete control, and God has counted everyone thoroughly, which is why there is no possibility of anyone escaping His grasp or any error occurring in the count. Then, everyone will present themselves entirely alone; neither their children, their forces, nor their partners and intercessors will be with them. A state of self-concern (nafsi-nafsi) will prevail, and everyone will have to answer for themselves before God.

إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ سَيَجْعَلُ لَهُمُ الرَّحْمَنُ وُدًّا۔ 

**The State of the Victorious Team:** Now, the mention is made of those who will remain safe from this state of helplessness and anxiety. It is stated: "As for those who lived a life of faith and righteous deeds, the Lord of Mercy will create an atmosphere of love and affection for them on all sides." Here the matter is stated briefly, but other places specify that on that day, the angels will welcome the believers with greetings of peace, peace will be sent to them from the Lord of Honor, and the believers themselves will congratulate one another like a victorious team. In short, the entire atmosphere will echo with words of welcome and praise!

**The Wisdom in Repeating the Attribute "Rahman":** In this set of verses, and indeed in this entire Surah, you must have felt that among the Beautiful Names, the name "Al-Rahman" (the Most Merciful) appears repeatedly. This name has not appeared in any other Surah of the Qur'an as many times as it has in this Surah. My mentor, Maulana Farahi, used to call this Surah a "Rahmani" Surah. Obviously, this is not without wisdom. In my view, the wisdom is that regarding the attributes of God and His treatment of His servants, nations have fallen into misguidance mostly due to a false concept of God's mercy. In this Surah, along with other matters, the false concept of mercy is reformed and its correct concept is provided, which is why the reference to Al-Rahman appears repeatedly.

**Misguidances Arising from a False Concept of Mercy:** I wish to point out some of the misguidances that a false concept of mercy has produced:

1. In this Surah, first the Christians are mentioned, so take their misguidance first. A major factor in their misguidance was their illusion that since human is an eternal and natural sinner, there is no way for his salvation. For this reason, the Lord of Mercy, out of His mercy, sent His son, who was sacrificed to become an atonement for the sins of all who believe in him.

2. The misguidance of the Jews is not directly under discussion in this Surah, but it is clear from the Qur'an that they too used God's mercy as a shield to cover their crimes. Their concept was: "We are the offspring of prophets and saints, which is why we will not be put into Hell at all, and even if we are, the Lord of Mercy will forgive us after a few days of warning."

3. The Arab mushrikeen worshipped the angels, making them the daughters of God. Their concept was that the matters of the Resurrection are merely imaginary, but if they contain any reality, these daughters of the Lord of Mercy will intercede and have them forgiven by their father.

**The Correct Concept of Mercy:** All these misconceptions and misguidances were in the background. Allah clarified the correct concept of His mercy to reform them: His mercy does not invalidate His justice; rather, His justice is a demand of His mercy. The glad tidings He gives to the righteous are a mercy, and the warnings He gives to the rebels are also His mercy. If He were to forgive the wrongdoers and rebels merely because they are the offspring of great people or prophets, or because someone interceded for them, or because they love His presumed son, this would not be mercy but open injustice. If God were to do so, He would not be the Lord of Mercy, but—refuge is with Allah—an unjust God. He is Al-Rahman, so the essential demand of His mercy is that He grant justice to the upright, rewarding their uprightness fully, and cast the wrongdoers and rebels into Hell. Casting the wrongdoers into Hell is not contrary to His mercy, but is the very manifestation of His mercy. Remember also that those who disobey God do not harm God; they harm themselves and the servants of God. God's boundaries are not for His own protection—He is above all protection—but for the protection and progress of the creation; therefore, the matter of criminals is not a personal matter of God, but a matter of His entire creation. The demand of His mercy towards His creation is that He not leave any criminal without punishment. Keep these demands of the attribute of Al-Rahman in mind and study this Surah with care; then this truth will become clear: that the warnings or glad tidings in it are all based on God's attribute of mercy.

فَإِنَّمَا يَسَّرْنَاهُ بِلِسَانِكَ لِتُبَشِّرَ بِهِ الْمُتَّقِينَ وَتُنْذِرَ بِهِ قَوْمًا لُدًّا۔

In "yassarnahu", the pronoun refers to the Qur'an. Although the reference is not explicitly mentioned in words, since the discussion from the beginning of the Surah has been that the call of all prophets has been the same as what the Qur'an presents, the Qur'an is implicitly under discussion. In such a context, bringing a pronoun without an explicit reference is not a defect but a demand of eloquence; this style serves as evidence of the high status of the reference, showing that although it is not mentioned, everyone knows it without mention. Examples of this are common in the Qur'an.

**The Meaning of "Taysir":** Most people have understood "taysir" to mean "making easy", and have taken the verse to mean that the Qur'an is exceptionally easy. Although it is true in itself that Allah has made the Qur'an easy, it is entirely incorrect to assume that the Qur'an is a flat thing requiring no thought, effort, or reflection. Those who have fallen into this misunderstanding have not understood the correct meaning of "taysir". In Arabic, its primary meaning is not to make easy, but to equip a thing with all its essentials and make it highly suitable and favorable for its intended purpose. If they say "yassara-farsa lir-rukoub", it means he equipped the horse with saddle, stirrups, and reins, preparing it for riding. In this way, "wa laqad yassarnal-Qur'ana lidh-dhikr" means: "We have made the Qur'an highly suitable, equipped with all essentials, and highly favorable for instruction and reminder." We have discussed the various aspects of the taysir of the Qur'an in the light of both literature and the Qur'an in our book *Mabadi Tadabbur-e-Qur'an*. Those seeking details may refer to it.

**"Qawman Ludda" refers to the Quraysh.** "Ludd" is the plural of "Aladd". "Aladd" means argumentative, obstinate, and stubborn. Due to their nomadic life, the Arabs were both harsh and, due to their unlettered state, highly rigid and prejudiced in religious matters, which is why they opposed the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) at every step and presented new demands daily to distress him.

**Solace to the Prophet and Warning to the Opponents:** Finally, this is solace to the Prophet and a warning to the opponents. It is stated: "We have made this Qur'an highly suitable, reasoned, and convincing from every aspect for instruction, reminder, and establishing proof. Furthermore, it is in your language and the language of your people, which you can understand well, and through which you can explain to your people, if they wish to understand. In its presence, nothing else is needed. Your responsibility regarding it is not to compel people to believe; your duty is only to give glad tidings of success and salvation to those who have fear of God. As for those who have rolled up their sleeves to argue, warn them of the punishment of both this world and the Hereafter." The same subject is expressed in Surah Ta-Ha as: "And thus We have sent it down as an Arabic Qur'an and have diversified therein the warnings that perhaps they will avoid [sin] or it would cause them remembrance." (Ta-Ha: 113)

وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُمْ مِنْ قَرْنٍ هَلْ تُحِسُّ مِنْهُمْ مِنْ أَحَدٍ أَوْ تَسْمَعُ لَهُمْ رِكْزًا۔ "Rikz" means a faint sound, whisper, or rustle.

The meaning is: "Discharge your duty of warning and giving glad tidings. Those who do not listen to you will face their own end. History contains ample lessons for them. How many generations before them have been seized by Our punishment; do you feel any trace of them today, or hear from them even a whisper! In the same manner, these people too will become nameless and traceless in retribution for denying you."

With these lines, the commentary of this Surah is completed. *O Allah, show us the truth as truth and grant us its following, and show us falsehood as falsehood and grant us its avoidance. And may the peace and blessings of Allah be upon Muhammad.*
